title: Pointer conversions should be restricted to a safe subset
description: Pointer conversions should be restricted to a safe subsetAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugbased-on-misraWhy is this an issue?More InfoCasting an object pointer can very easily lead to undefined behavior. Only a few cases are supported, for instance casting an object pointer to a large enough integral type (and back again), casting an object pointer to a pointer to void (and back again)…​ Using a pointer cast to access an object as if it was of another type than its real type is not supported in general.This rule detects casts between object pointers and incompatible types.Noncompliant code examplestruct S1 *p1; struct S2; void f () { (float) p1; // Noncompliant, conversion to floating point type (int *) p1; // Noncompliant float f; int *i = (int *)&f; // Noncompliant, u
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Pointer conversions should be restricted to a safe subset. Casting to incompatible types or floating point can cause undefined behavior.
